The result is that the cost of underwriting risk should be compressed to the cost of programming an open ledger to handle compliance and risk management (plus the necessary on- and off-boarding costs), minus the yields generated by the stablecoin collateral. This is an objective structural advantage over existing correspondent banking solutions, as well as other modern cross-border solutions such as Wise that rely on closed, centralized databases.

Perhaps most importantly, unlike domestic payments, governments appear to have little incentive to build globally interoperable payment infrastructure themselves, which would cannibalize the value proposition of stablecoins. In fact, I think there is a strong structural incentive for governments not to build interoperable payment rails, in order to keep value held primarily in their national currencies.

This is perhaps the most favorable tailwind for stablecoins - cross-border payments are a unique public market problem that is seeking a private market solution. As long as governments have a structural incentive to maintain a poor global payment infrastructure, stablecoins will remain well-positioned to gradually facilitate global commerce and shrink the $2 trillion in annual cross-border payment fees.

Path to Adoption

Finally, let’s speculate on the path to adoption. Ultimately, two factors will determine the adoption arc of stablecoins:

●Payment type (i.e. B2B, B2C, C2C, etc.)

●Payment corridor (i.e. G7, G20 small countries, long-tail customers)

Intuitively, payment corridors with the highest fees and the worst banking/payment infrastructure will likely be the first to adopt stablecoins (e.g. Global South, Latin America, Southeast Asia). Additionally, these regions tend to be the ones that suffer from irresponsible monetary policies and historically volatile national currencies. Adopting stablecoins in these regions has a dual benefit: cheaper fees and access to USD. The latter is arguably the biggest driver of stablecoin demand in these regions right now, and is likely to continue to be so.

Secondly, given that businesses have historically been more cost-sensitive than consumers, B2B use cases will also lead adoption via the previous factor. Today, more than 90% of cross-border payments are B2B. Within this vertical, SMEs appear to be best suited to adopt stablecoins, as they operate on thinner margins while also being more willing to take risks than larger enterprises. SMEs that do not have access to traditional banking infrastructure and require USD appear to be the sweet spot for stablecoin adoption. Other notable use cases for stablecoins globally include treasury management, trade finance, international payments, and accounts receivable.

Going forward, as long-tail customers increasingly adopt stablecoins as a structurally superior cross-border payment method, we should see others gradually follow suit as the structural advantages become too obvious to ignore.

5Speculation

The final point is perhaps the most obvious and straightforward. Humans have an innate desire to speculate and gamble. This has been the case for thousands of years and will continue to be so.

Furthermore, it is becoming increasingly clear that blockchain is uniquely positioned to fill this gap. As a programmable asset ledger, blockchain once again lowers the bar for issuing assets—in this case, speculative assets with nonlinear returns. This includes everything from perpetual contracts to prediction markets to mem coins.

Looking forward, as users tend to take risks and seek increasingly nonlinear outcomes, blockchains appear well positioned to meet this need, providing increasingly novel means of speculation. This could include markets for anything from athletes, musicians, songs, social trends, to TikTok posts.

Humanity will continue to demand new ways to speculate, and blockchain is the best first principles means of satisfying this need.

6. What’s the future like?

Throughout history, adoption of new technologies has followed a similar arc:

An emerging technology offers a structural advantage -> A small group of businesses adopt the technology to improve profit margins -> Incumbents either follow suit to remain competitive or cede market share to more nimble adopters -> Adoption of the new technology becomes a baseline requirement, as capital inherently chooses winners.

This is why, in my opinion, the adoption of blockchain as a programmable asset ledger is not only possible, but inevitable.By offering clear structural advantages in these five areas - tokenization, DeVin, DePin, payments, and speculation - it is likely only a matter of time before blockchain adoption occurs. While it is unclear how long it will take, what is certain is that we have never been closer to that goal.

data.title: US SEC Confirms Bitcoin and Dogecoin Mining Under Proof-of-Work Do Not Violate Securities Laws
data.description:
SEC Confirms Bitcoin Mining Does Not Violate US Securities Laws

The United States Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has clarified its stance on proof-of-work (PoW) mining, confirming that the activity does not fall under securities regulations.

The latest guidance puts to rest concerns about whether Bitcoin miners need to register their operations with the regulator, stating that mining does not involve "the offer and sale of securities."

JUST IN: 🇺🇸 The SEC confirms that #Bitcoin and Proof of Work mining are not securities under US law. 👏 pic.twitter.com/nWNbxHPI9F

— Simply Bitcoin (@SimplyBitcoinTV) March 20, 2025

Regulatory Clarity for Proof-of-Work Miners

The SEC's latest statement seeks to eliminate confusion surrounding the legal status of cryptocurrency mining.

Proof-of-work, which underpins Bitcoin and other digital assets, relies on miners using computational power to solve cryptographic puzzles, validate transactions, and secure blockchain networks.

In return, miners receive newly minted digital coins as rewards.

Source: coincodex.com

Concerns over regulatory classification had lingered, particularly as the previous SEC administration scrutinized proof-of-stake (PoS) networks.

Under former Chair Gary Gensler, the regulator had argued that staking—where crypto holders earn passive income by pledging assets to secure a network—could meet the Howey test criteria, making such tokens securities.

However, the SEC now draws a clear distinction between the two mechanisms.

The SEC said in its statement,

"By adding its computational resources to the network, the miner merely is engaging in an administrative or ministerial activity to secure the network, validate transactions and add new blocks, and receive rewards."

Miners Not Reliant on 'Efforts of Others'

A key factor in the SEC’s determination is that PoW mining does not satisfy the "efforts of others" condition of the Howey test, which is used to determine whether an asset is a security.

Since miners operate independently—rather than depending on a third party to generate profits—the SEC confirmed that their activities do not fall under its jurisdiction.

The guidance applies to both solo miners and mining pools, which combine computational resources for a higher chance of earning rewards.

The regulator made no direct mention of specific cryptocurrencies, but Bitcoin and Dogecoin are the two largest PoW assets by market capitalisation.

🚨 Top Proof of Work (PoW) Coins by Market Cap! 🚨@Bitcoin $1.58T | $79,818 (-3.1%)@dogecoin $23.16B | $0.1561 (-10.0%)@litecoin $6.63B | $87.70 (-8.9%)@bitcoincashclub $6.60B | $332.59 (-7.9%)@monero $3.71B | $201.76 (-3.5%)$ETC $2.56B | $16.96 (-5.6%)@KaspaCurrency… pic.twitter.com/KYIQNSMl37

— TokenHunter (@_TokenHunter) March 11, 2025

Ethereum, which originally ran on proof-of-work, transitioned to proof-of-stake in 2022.

Relief for Crypto Mining Industry

The announcement is expected to ease concerns within the crypto mining sector, particularly among large-scale operations that had faced uncertainty under past SEC leadership.

Bitcoin mining firms such as Marathon Digital (MARA), Riot Platforms (RIOT), and Bitfarms (BITF) may see renewed confidence from investors as a result.

For years, miners have expressed frustration over regulatory ambiguity.

The latest SEC guidance offers a more stable outlook, allowing proof-of-work miners to operate without the risk of being classified under securities laws.

New SEC Leadership and Policy Shift

The SEC’s approach to cryptocurrency has seen a notable change following the election of President Donald Trump.

The new leadership has moved to clarify rules for the industry while dropping several lawsuits and investigations initiated under the previous administration.

Meanwhile, attention now turns to the Senate Banking Committee, where Paul Atkins, Trump’s nominee for SEC Chair, is set to appear for a nomination hearing next Thursday.

🚨NEW: @realDonaldTrump’s pick for @SECGov chair Paul Atkins will face the @BankingGOP next Thursday for his nomination hearing. Trump’s pick for @USOCC, Jonathan Gould, will also have his hearing. pic.twitter.com/Ne91g0js9x

— Eleanor Terrett (@EleanorTerrett) March 20, 2025

If approved by the committee, Atkins’ nomination will proceed to a full Senate vote for confirmation.

data.title: Trump: The United States will dominate cryptocurrencies and next-generation financial technology
data.description:
Author: Weilin, PANews

Tonight, Trumpsaidat the Digital Asset Summitthat the United States will lead the development of cryptocurrencies and next-generation financial technologies.

According to Jinshi, US President Trump said: The United States is already ahead in the fields of cryptocurrency and next-generation financial technology. We are ending the last government’s regulatory war on cryptocurrencies and Bitcoin.

Of course, Trump has done more than that. Let us review his encryption policies since he took office.

This article will take stock of these significant crypto regulatory policy initiatives according to different policy divisions, and interpret their far-reaching impact on the crypto industry.

Trump signs crypto executive order

On January 23, the third day after U.S. President Trump took office, he signed the crypto executive order "Strengthening U.S. Leadership in Digital Financial Technology", proposing the establishment of a "Presidential Digital Asset Market Working Group" to explore federal regulatory measures for stablecoins and related plans for national digital asset reserves, and explicitly prohibiting the "establishment, issuance, circulation or use" of central bank digital currencies (CBDCs).

SEC Chairman changes, major adjustments to multiple regulatory strategies

Last July, at the Bitcoin 2024 conference held in Nashville, Trump delivered a speech in which he promised to remove SEC Chairman Gary Gensler, who was criticized by the encryption industry, on his first day in office.

On November 22, 2024, the SEC announced that Gary Gensler will step down on the first day of Trump's presidency. He officially stepped down on January 20 this year. His successor is Paul Atkins, CEO of Patomak Global Partners LLC and former SEC commissioner, and the nomination is currently awaiting congressional confirmation.

On January 22, the SEC immediately established an encryption task force and began to adjust its regulatory strategy, reducing the team that was responsible for cryptocurrency law enforcement actions and transferring some lawyers. The SEC also launched a website for the Cryptocurrency Task Force, where the head of the task force, Hester Peirce, listed ten priority tasks, focusing on the classification and regulation of crypto assets.

On January 24, the SEC announced in its latest Staff Accounting Bulletin No. 122 that it would withdraw the crypto accounting policy SAB 121, which had been criticized by the crypto industry. SAB 121 (Staff Accounting Bulletin No. 121) requires digital asset custodians to treat digital assets as liabilities and list them on the balance sheet at fair value. There are widespread concerns in the cryptocurrency industry that it could prevent banks from custodying digital assets and exclude them from the crypto market.

In addition, on May 22 last year, the FIT21 bill was passed by the House of Representatives, which was regarded as a historic breakthrough in the US encryption industry. The bill, which resolves the long-standing disagreement between the SEC and the CFTC over cryptocurrency regulation, is currently being advanced.

SEC withdraws collective lawsuit against crypto companies

On February 27, the SEC terminated its investigation into Gemini Trust and did not take any enforcement action. Prior to this, the SEC had withdrawn its lawsuit against Coinbase and terminated its investigations into OpenSea, Robinhood and Uniswap. In the seventh week of Trump's presidency (March 3-March 9), the SEC agreed to drop the lawsuit against Kraken without paying any fines or admitting any violations, and Kraken's business model was not affected.

Redefine "exchange"

On March 11, the SEC is evaluating a proposal to redefine "exchange", which may provide clearer guidance for the regulatory framework for US crypto trading platforms.

At the same time, the U.S. House of Representatives passed a resolution to overturn the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) broker rules for decentralized finance (DeFi) platforms. The rule requires crypto entities to collect specific taxpayer and transaction information, which is difficult for DeFi platforms to enforce. Previously, the U.S. Senate had voted to pass the resolution, but due to budget rules, it still needs to be voted on again before it can be sent to President Trump for signature.

Pardon Silk Road founder Ross Ulbricht

On January 22, Trump fulfilled another promise made at the Bitcoin 2024 conference and pardoned Silk Road founder Ross Ulbricht, who was sentenced to life in prison without parole. Ross Ulbricht later tweeted his gratitude to Trump, who released him after 11 years in prison.

Appointment of crypto-friendly officials

On January 20, after the presidential inauguration ceremony, the White House stated that the new President Trump who was sworn in had appointed Republican member Mark Uyeda as acting chairman of the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission SEC. Earlier, Trump announced that he would nominate Paul Atkins as SEC chairman.

In the second week of Trump's presidency, the Senate confirmed his nominee for new Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent. The financial tycoon is open to cryptocurrencies.

In the fourth week, Trump nominated Brian Quintenz, a former CFTC commissioner and executive of Kalshi, an event betting market, as the new head of the regulatory agency.

In the fifth week, billionaire Howard Lutnick was confirmed as the next Secretary of Commerce, and the market immediately began to pay attention to how he would affect the regulatory environment of the crypto industry.

Both the Senate and the House of Representatives also have crypto-friendly officials holding important positions. On January 23, the Senate Banking Committee established a Digital Assets Committee, chaired by Senator Cynthia Lummis, to promote industry compliance. On March 3, the Republican leader of the U.S. House of Representatives and Congressman Ritchie Torres are jointly establishing the "Congressional Crypto Caucus" to promote legislation that is beneficial to the crypto industry and form a voting coalition in support of digital assets in the lower house of Congress.

Officially announced strategic Bitcoin reserves and digital asset reserves

In the sixth week of his presidency (February 24-March 2), Trump announced five major encryption strategic reserve categories on the social media platform. The United States' cryptocurrency strategic reserves will include five major asset categories: BTC, ETH, XRP, SOL, and ADA. The selection of ADA has sparked controversy, and some market participants have dubbed it an “advertising space.” But on March 7, AI and cryptocurrency czar David Sacks said that ADA, SOL, and XRP were mentioned because they are the top five cryptocurrencies by market capitalization.

In the morning of March 7th, Beijing time, the strategic Bitcoin reserves promised by Trump arrived! David Sacks announced on the X platform that US President Trump has officially signed an executive order to establish a strategic Bitcoin reserve and digital asset reserve. However, since both reserves rely mainly on “proceeds from criminal or civil asset forfeiture” for funding, the prices of tokens such as BTC in the market reacted negatively in the short term, and then recovered slightly.

In addition to the president's executive order, in terms of congressional legislation, on March 12, U.S. Senator Cynthia Lummis resubmitted the Bitcoin Act (Boosting Innovation, Technology, and Competitiveness through Optimized Investment Nationwide Act of 2025) to the 119th Congress. The bill will allow the U.S. government to hold more than 1 million bitcoins. The bill was originally proposed in July 2024 and requires the U.S. government to purchase 200,000 bitcoins each year for five years, with funds coming from adjustments to existing funds from the Federal Reserve and the Treasury Department. Under the amendment, the U.S. government can hold additional bitcoins through lawful means, including civil or criminal forfeiture, donation, or transfer to federal agencies.

Digital Asset Summit

In the third week of Trump's inauguration (February 3-February 9), David Sacks and several U.S. lawmakers held the first press conference on digital assets on Capitol Hill, elaborating on the latest plans of the White House and Congress to implement the development of digital assets in the United States. Sacks said at the meeting that he looked forward to working with congressional lawmakers and announced in a high-profile manner that he wanted to "create a golden age of digital assets."

On March 7 local time, the United States held its first White House Digital Asset Summit, and President Trump delivered a brief speech at the summit. He said, “Last year, I pledged to make the United States a global Bitcoin superpower and the crypto capital of the world. We are taking historic action to deliver on that promise,and suggested: "From today, the United States will follow a rule that every Bitcoin holder knows well - never sell your Bitcoin."

Trump mentioned that he would end the "Stranglehold 2.0" against the crypto industry under the Biden administration. However, despite news from on-site that the summit was recognized by industry leaders, the meeting did not bring about an increase in the prices of assets such as Bitcoin and Ethereum, and the cryptocurrency market fell significantly after the summit.

The market is seeing a wave of applications for crypto ETFs

As of March 12, the tokens that have applied for ETFs include at least DOGE, LTC, HEAR, SOL, XRP, SUI, AVAX, DOT, LINK, ADA, APT, AXL, etc. According to Bloomberg analysts James Seyffart and Eric Balchunas, the current market probability of approval for LTC, DOGE, SOL and XRP spot ETFs is relatively high. The market's expectations for other mainstream crypto asset ETFs to be launched on the U.S. capital market have clearly increased.

Due to significant personnel changes at the SEC, its policies have become more friendly to cryptocurrencies. If the United States launches an altcoin ETF, it may directly lead to imitation by other countries and regions in the world. Bloomberg analysts expect the SEC to make a decision on the proposed altcoin ETF in October this year.

“Debanking” has aroused widespread discussion

On the evening of February 5, the U.S. Senate Banking, Housing and Urban Affairs Committee held a hearing on the theme of “Investigating the real impact of debanking on the United States.” Witnesses included Anchorage Digital co-founder and CEO Nathan McCauley, Davis Wright Tremaine LLP partner Stephen Gannon, Old Glory Bank president and CEO Mike Ring, and Brookings Institution senior fellow in economic studies Aaron Klein. The hearing explored the impact of bank account closures and financial service restrictions on businesses and individuals, and studied relevant policy responses.

At a Senate Banking Committee hearing on February 11, local time, Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell said that in view of the criticism that the crypto industry has been excluded from banking services, it is time to "re-examine" the issue of de-banking. Senate Banking Committee Chairman Tim Scott, R-S.C., asked Powell if he agreed to commit to working with lawmakers to end disbanking; Powell agreed. It is expected that the discussion on "de-banking" will be further developed this year.

Showed great interest in Bitcoin reserves

As of March 4, 24 U.S. states have proposed draft crypto reserve bills. Most states' bills are still in the draft proposal or House review stage. A few states have made rapid progress (such as Texas and Utah), while relevant bills in five states (Pennsylvania, Montana, North Dakota, Wyoming, and South Dakota) have been rejected. The reasons for the veto were concerns about the risks and volatility associated with digital assets, concerns about the risk to taxpayer funds, the high energy consumption of cryptocurrency mining, and the possibility that digital currencies could be used for illegal activities.

Texas, which is at the forefront, has previously passed SB 21, which stipulates that a fund managed by the state government will be created to hold Bitcoin and other cryptocurrencies. The Texas comptroller will oversee the reserve, which will hold cryptocurrencies with a market value of at least $500 billion and be eligible for state budget allocations.

Legislation around the stablecoin regulatory framework

On February 5, U.S. Senator Bill Hagerty proposed the Stablecoin Regulatory Act (GENIUS Act), which would incorporate stablecoins such as USDT and USDC into the Federal Reserve's regulatory framework and provide compliance operational guidance. As of March 12, the U.S. Senate has updated the bill, which specifically expands “reciprocal provisions for stablecoin payments in overseas jurisdictions.”

At the White House summit, Trump instructed his policy implementers to push for stablecoin legislation and planned to complete it before the August congressional recess. The original goal was to submit legislation within the first 100 days of his term, but that timeline has now been extended by four months.

Conclusion

In general, in the eight weeks since Trump took office, a series of major adjustments have taken place in US encryption regulation, from policy direction to key personnel changes, all pointing to a more open regulatory environment. Can the United States really become the world's cryptocurrency capital as Trump says? Policy uncertainty remains and market reactions are relatively cautious, so future regulatory trends still require continued attention.

data.title: Dogecoin’s Decade of Giving: The Meme Coin That Redefined Philanthropy and Its Charity Evolution
data.description:
Dogecoin: The Meme That Keeps on Giving

Dogecoin (DOGE) has come a long way since its inception as a meme coin in 2013.

What began as a joke inspired by the Shiba Inu internet meme has transformed into one of the world’s most recognised and actively traded cryptocurrencies.

While its origins were lighthearted, Dogecoin’s evolution is far more than just a viral trend—it has become a digital asset with real-world utility, from everyday transactions to charitable giving.

Dogecoin's philanthropic journey began just a month after its launch in January 2014, and over the past decade, it has grown into a global force for charitable initiatives.

1/🚀 十年之后：大慈大悲狗狗币

2013年12月，#dogecoin 狗狗币诞生，如今11岁的它已是比肩 #btc 的传奇，更是 #memecoins 带头大狗。

2014年1月，刚满月的狗狗进入慈善事业。十年之后，当年的小狗狗也成长为全球慈善先锋。… pic.twitter.com/PmszC9CMIl

— 狗霸大大 (@tugouswap) November 5, 2024

What truly sets Dogecoin apart is not its technology or even its beloved mascot—it is the passionate community behind it.

Known as "shibes," Dogecoin enthusiasts have embedded generosity into the coin’s ethos, using it as a tool for positive change.

From disaster relief efforts to funding animal welfare organisations, the Dogecoin community has demonstrated the power of collective generosity.

Their commitment to giving back has inspired others to contribute, proving that a cryptocurrency can be more than just a financial instrument—it can be a vehicle for meaningful impact.

Let’s explore Dogecoin’s most significant milestones in philanthropy.

20 January 2014: Dogecoin Supports the Jamaican Bobsled Team

In a remarkable display of generosity, Dogecoin supporters raised over $25,000 to help the Jamaican bobsleigh team compete at the 2014 Winter Olympics in Sochi.

In a perfect convergence of buzzwords, Dogecoin donations send the Jamaican Bobsled Team to Sochi. #olympicdreampic.twitter.com/dv3r0e7jVb

— NOIZGewitter (@NOIZGewitter) January 21, 2014

This event marked the first time the world truly recognised Dogecoin’s potential as a force for crowdfunding and community-driven philanthropy.

The Jamaican team had qualified for the Olympics for the first time since 2002, with Winston Watt, a 46-year-old Jamaican-American and former Olympian, set to pilot the two-man sled alongside brakeman Marvin Dixon.

However, financial constraints threatened their participation.

Despite investing his own funds to cover training expenses, Watt revealed they still lacked the estimated $40,000 needed to travel to Russia.

Liam Butler, who runs the Dogecoin foundation along with the currency's initial creators Jackson Palmer and Billy Markus, explained:

"We started without a concrete plan in mind. I sent a few emails out… but that was the extent of it."

In response, a fundraiser called "Dogesled" was launched, and within hours, the Dogecoin community had contributed over 26 million Dogecoins—so much that the coin’s value surged by 50% against Bitcoin in just 12 hours.

Dogecoin is sponsoring the Jamacian Bobsled team for the olympics. This is my favorite news of the day.

— Rachel Fee (@RachFee) January 20, 2014

He expressed:

"Myself and Jackson Palmer (the creator of Dogecoin) were at a local pub trivia in Sydney when we noticed the value of Dogecoin had more than doubled since we'd last checked so we raced back to my house to ensure we could get the best price for the donations in a form the team could actually use. As much as we have faith in Dogecoin to become the community currency of the internet, we still understand that the team need to buy their airfares in a fiat currency."

Their collective efforts ensured Watt and Dixon could represent their country on the world stage.

This generosity exemplified Dogecoin’s identity as the “tipping currency” of the internet.

At today’s prices, those 26 million Dogecoins would be worth $4.5 million, highlighting just how far the cryptocurrency has come.

22 March 2014: World Water Day – Turning Dogecoin’s Virtual Faucet into a Real One

The Dogecoin community's philanthropic spirit shone through with the #Doge4Water campaign, which raised over $30,000 to provide clean drinking water for communities in Kenya.

But why did the Dogecoin Foundation choose this cause?

Many early Dogecoin users received their first coins from community “faucets,” a playful way to distribute Dogecoin to newcomers.

This sparked an idea: could the concept of faucets translate into real-world impact by bringing clean water to those in need?

Rallying around the cause, the community raised 40 million Dogecoins—worth approximately $31,000 at the time—to fund the construction of two clean water wells and essential infrastructure in Kenya.

The Dogecoins were converted into fiat currency and directed toward the project.

A tweet from an anonymous user, @savethemhood, helped propel the campaign past its 40-million-Dogecoin goal, surpassing expectations well before the targeted deadline of 22 March, World Water Day.

@tipdoge tip @Doge4water 14000000 may we all drink water. let the wealthy fill your cup. #savethemhood

— Hood (@savethemhood) March 16, 2014

The success of #Doge4Water underscored the power of the Dogecoin community to turn digital generosity into tangible, life-changing outcomes.

April 2014: Sponsoring NASCAR Driver Josh Wise

Building on the momentum of sending Jamaica's bobsled team to the Winter Olympics, the Dogecoin community turned its attention to the NASCAR world, raising 67 million Dogecoin to sponsor a car at the iconic Talladega Superspeedway.

The effort began when members of Reddit's NASCAR community identified up-and-coming driver Josh Wise as an underdog in need of financial backing.

In just eight days, 1,200 donors contributed an average of $41 each, successfully raising $55,000 to cover the cost of the sponsorship.

Moolah, a cryptocurrency payment platform, helped convert the donations into US dollars at a favourable exchange rate, ensuring the entire amount could be used.

Wise has expressed:

"I can't thank the dogecoin and Reddit community enough for the support."

He then quoted what has become an unofficial slogan of the Dogecoin community:

"To the moon!"

The car, dubbed the "#98 Moonrocket," made its debut at the Aarons 499 Sprint Series Cup Race on 4th May, with Dogecoin and the Reddit alien adorning its exterior.

This sponsorship not only elevated Dogecoin’s visibility but also highlighted the community's creativity and unity.

Shortly after, Wise secured a primary sponsor, marking the success of the Dogecoin mission.

Almost 10 years ago I was voted into the @nascar All Star race by the @dogecoin and @reddit communities! pic.twitter.com/BwbDXMmjMI

— Josh Wise (@Josh_Wise) July 24, 2023

In 2016, as a gesture of gratitude, Wise continued to wear a Dogecoin-themed helmet during races.

2015: Supporting the WaterAid Project & More

In 2015, the Dogecoin Foundation partnered with WaterAid and other organisations committed to improving water resource management across Africa.

Through collaboration with the Dogecoin community, they helped expand access to clean drinking water, turning digital donations into meaningful global impact.

December 2018: Supporting Post-Disaster Relief Efforts

The Dogecoin Foundation collaborated with the Red Cross to aid post-disaster relief efforts, leveraging its community-driven spirit to provide critical support in times of crisis.

March 2021: Supporting Humanitarian Aid efforts in Ukraine

Amidst the Russia-Ukraine conflict, the Dogecoin community rallied to support humanitarian aid efforts in Ukraine, demonstrating its commitment to global solidarity and social impact.

2022 Through 2023

Following its revitalisation, the Dogecoin Foundation initiated a series of community-driven charitable projects, inviting global participation to drive meaningful change.

Focusing on environmental sustainability, education, and social welfare, the foundation continues to foster collective action for a better future.

The #dogecoin community raised 163428 Dogecoin and removed around 42,000 pounds of trash from the ocean. @teamseas@mrbeast@markrober

Thank You #DogeCommunity.

Much Wow! pic.twitter.com/rEUvvwJvBR

— Dogecoin (@dogecoin) January 5, 2022

March 2025: Dogecoin Takes the Track in Upcoming Indianapolis 500

The Dogecoin Foundation, in collaboration with House of Doge, has partnered with IndyCar driver Devlin DeFrancesco and Rahal Letterman Lanigan Racing to launch the Dogecoin Indy 500 Voting and Donation Platform.

This initiative brings Dogecoin to the iconic Indianapolis 500, set for its 109th running on 25 May.

Fans will have the unique opportunity to vote on DeFrancesco’s car design, featuring the beloved Dogecoin Shiba Inu, and participate in a fan-powered contest to select his official race car wrap and helmet.

The three design options—Blaze, Turbowave, and BananaBoost—will be open for voting from 19 March to 25 March, with participants also entering to win a Dogecoin wallet loaded with $1,000.

DOGECOIN TAKES THE WHEEL: INDY 500 SPONSORSHIP WITH COMMUNITY-CHOSEN CAR WRAP!🏎️🏁

Hey Dogecoin community, get ready to start your engines—Dogecoin is sponsoring IndyCar driver Devlin DeFrancesco for the legendary Indy 500 race, and YOU get to pick the car’s wrap design.

The…

— REDWRITER (@The_REDWRITER) March 19, 2025

Beyond the excitement of the race, this partnership carries a strong philanthropic mission.

DeFrancesco, a dedicated Dogecoin supporter and dog lover, has pledged to donate $25,000 in Dogecoin to Riley Children’s Foundation, which supports Riley Children’s Health, a leading pediatric care center.

He has also committed to receiving $100,000 of his salary in Dogecoin, marking a significant step toward crypto adoption in professional sports.

The campaign encourages the Dogecoin community to "race toward generosity" by contributing to Riley’s in Dogecoin.

Those who donate will have a chance to win an exclusive collector’s prize, turning their participation into a meaningful impact on children’s health.

As the Dogecoin community gears up for this historic event, they are not only making motorsport history but also driving change for a worthy cause.
